originalAlarm.description: `__curr_1644` could be null (null value originating from line 2678) and is dereferenced.

sanitizedDescription: `curr` could be null (null value originating from line 2678) and is dereferenced.
model:
(define-fun __need_malloc_and_calloc () Bool
  false)
(define-fun __STRICT_ANSI__ () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_FEATURE_LS_SORTFILES () Bool
  true)
(define-fun _STDLIB_H () Bool
  false)
reasoning: 2631486f1bf.c L66: curr could be null (from malloc) and is dereferenced. Same underlying bug as T74/T99 etc. (curr null deref from malloc in CONFIG_FEATURE_LS_SORTFILES sorting path). PC: CONFIG_FEATURE_LS_SORTFILES AND !_STDLIB_H AND !__need_malloc_and_calloc. The restrictive !_STDLIB_H over-constrains the PC (bug also occurs in normal compilation with _STDLIB_H=T). !__need_malloc_and_calloc is non-restrictive. New bug, over-constrained PC.
